prompt: I want to rename a Terraform resource from aws_security_group.instance to aws_security_group.web_server. Will this destroy and recreate the security group?
expected: Skill activates and warns that without a moved block Terraform interprets the rename as destroy-old + create-new; add a moved { from = aws_security_group.instance; to = aws_security_group.web_server } block so plan shows a move with no destroy/create, then verify with terraform plan before applying.
notes: rationale: Direct rename question — without a moved block, yes it destroys. This is the core scenario.; source id: mb-si-01

prompt: I'm refactoring my Terraform code to move a bunch of resources from the root module into a module called webserver_cluster. How do I do this without destroying my production infrastructure?
expected: Skill activates and prescribes making the code change first, then adding one moved block per resource mapping the root address to the new module address (moved { from = aws_type.name; to = module.webserver_cluster.aws_type.name }); confirm plan shows only moves and keep the blocks until every environment has applied.
notes: rationale: Module extraction refactor — the primary use case for moved blocks.; source id: mb-si-02

prompt: What's the difference between using a moved block and using terraform state mv to rename a resource?
expected: Skill activates and explains both achieve the same state remapping, but moved blocks are declarative, code-reviewable, stackable, and applied via normal plan/apply, whereas terraform state mv is imperative and leaves no artifact; recommend moved blocks on Terraform 1.1+, reserving state mv for pre-1.1 or cross-state-file moves.
notes: rationale: Directly asks the moved block vs. terraform state mv comparison — the skill explains both and recommends moved blocks.; source id: mb-si-03

prompt: I'm migrating IAM users from count to for_each. The state currently has aws_iam_user.example[0], [1], [2]. After the migration I want aws_iam_user.example["alice"], ["bob"], ["carol"]. How do I do this without destroying the users?
expected: Skill activates and prescribes one moved block per instance mapping each positional key to its identity key (moved { from = aws_iam_user.example[0]; to = aws_iam_user.example["alice"] }, etc.); confirm plan shows only moves with no destroy/create.
notes: rationale: Count-to-for_each state key migration — the stacked moved block use case.; source id: mb-si-04

prompt: How do I delete a resource from Terraform state without destroying the real infrastructure?
notes: rationale: terraform state rm — removing from state entirely is different from renaming/moving.; source id: mb-sni-01

prompt: How do I import an existing AWS resource into Terraform state?
notes: rationale: terraform import or import blocks — adding new resources to state, not renaming existing ones.; source id: mb-sni-02

prompt: I want to move my Terraform state file from one S3 bucket to another. How do I do that?
notes: rationale: Moving the entire state file between backends — terraform init -migrate-state, not moved blocks.; source id: mb-sni-03

prompt: I want to split one large Terraform module into two separate modules with their own state files. Some resources in the first module need to move to the second. Can I use a moved block for this?
expected: Skill answers no: moved blocks only remap addresses within the same state file, so a cross-state-file move requires terraform state mv (or state pull/push / import) to relocate the resource into the second module's state; it should surface this boundary explicitly.
notes: rationale: Cross-state-file move — the skill's boundary explicitly says moved blocks don't work across state files; terraform state mv is required.; source id: mb-bb-01

prompt: I applied a moved block to rename a resource and it worked. Can I remove the moved block now?
expected: Skill answers not yet: the moved block must persist until every environment (staging, production, DR) has applied the plan containing it; removing it early makes Terraform plan a new create at the old address for any environment that hasn't applied — remove it only in a follow-up commit once all are up to date.
notes: rationale: Premature moved block removal — the skill's lifecycle guidance: only remove after all environments have applied.; source id: mb-bb-02

prompt: I'm on Terraform 1.0. I want to rename a resource without destroying it. What are my options?
expected: Skill notes moved blocks require Terraform 1.1+, so on 1.0 the only option is the imperative terraform state mv (which remaps state but leaves no reviewable code artifact); it may suggest upgrading to 1.1+ to use declarative moved blocks going forward.
notes: rationale: Version boundary — moved blocks require Terraform 1.1+. On 1.0, terraform state mv is the only option.; source id: mb-bb-03
